issues.criterion: npm run lint:js passes with zero errors
issues.details: The .eslintrc.js configuration file is completely missing from the repository root. Running `npm run lint:js` fails with: 'ESLint couldn't find a configuration file'. This means the JS lint step of the CI pipeline cannot execute at all. The phase3-blocks.md documentation incorrectly states 'All code passes npm run lint (eslint + stylelint) with zero errors'.
issues.remediation: Add a standard AEM boilerplate .eslintrc.js to the repo root. The standard boilerplate includes 'airbnb-base' extends with @babel/eslint-parser. Note: when a valid .eslintrc.js is provided, several blocks also show no-param-reassign violations (breadcrumbs, cards, content-filter, embed, fragment, hubspot-form, icon-grid, quote, search, stats) that must also be fixed.

issues.criterion: AEM preview pages render and are visually comparable to source
issues.details: All AEM preview and live endpoints return HTTP 404. Admin API shows code.status=404 (code bus not synced) and preview/live status=404 (no DA content published). URLs checked: https://main--eds-migration--manuel-vara.aem.page/ and https://main--eds-migration--manuel-vara.aem.live/ both return 404. fstab.yaml at aem.live is also unreachable. Playwright verification confirmed all pages at 404.
issues.remediation: Install the AEM CodeSync GitHub app on the manuel-vara/eds-migration repository to enable code sync to the AEM CDN. Then publish content to DA (Document Authoring) at content.da.live/manuel-vara/eds-migration so preview pages become available.

issues.criterion: Visual regression: screenshot pairs are mandatory per verifier rules
issues.details: No preview screenshots could be captured for any archetype because all AEM preview URLs return 404. The verifier rules require per-archetype screenshot pairs (source-bundle vs preview) at desktop (>=1280px), tablet (~768px), and mobile (<=420px). Zero preview screenshots exist — this is a mandatory FAIL condition per the verifier's visual rule.
issues.remediation: Once the AEM preview is available (see code sync issue above), re-run this verifier to capture and compare screenshot pairs for all 28 archetypes. Source bundle screenshots are available at .eds-migration/state/source-bundle/pages/<slug>/desktop.png and mobile.png.

issues.criterion: CSS is properly scoped: every selector starts with .{block-name}
issues.details: modal.css begins with a `body.modal-open { overflow: hidden; }` selector that is scoped to body, not to `.modal`. While this is a common EDS pattern for scroll locking, it is technically outside the `.modal` block scope and can affect the entire page.
issues.remediation: This is acceptable EDS practice but worth noting. Alternatively, use `document.body.classList.add('modal-open')` with a `:has(.modal[open])` selector in styles.css to maintain scoping.

evidence.finding: PASS: fstab.yaml content is 'mountpoints:\n  /: https://content.da.live/manuel-vara/eds-migration'. Correct DA mountpoint confirmed. However, the file returns 404 from aem.live (code sync not set up).

PASS: All 18 blueprint blocks found on main branch with both .js and .css files: accordion, breadcrumbs, cards, carousel, columns, content-filter, embed, fragment, hero, hubspot-form, modal, profile, quote, search, stats, table, tabs, video. One extra block (icon-grid) is present but not required by blueprint.
PASS: All 18 blocks confirmed to have exactly one `export default` function (decorate). content-filter and hubspot-form export async decorate functions.

PASS: scripts.js implements the standard EDS loading pipeline (loadEager→loadLazy→loadDelayed). Auto-blocks include buildHeroBlock (h1+preceding picture) and buildBreadcrumbsBlock (skips homepage/index). Button decoration handles strong=primary, em=secondary, strong+em=accent. loadFonts with sessionStorage optimization.

PASS: CSS custom properties include --color-navy: #002855 (Almac navy) and --color-teal: #00857c (Almac teal). All five section styles implemented: .grey, .dark, .cta-band, .full-width-image, .sidebar.

evidence.render_strategy: git clone + npm install + npm run lint
evidence.finding: FAIL: npm run lint:js FAILS — .eslintrc.js is absent from repo root. Error: 'ESLint couldn't find a configuration file'. npm run lint:css PASSES with 0 errors (stylelint with stylelint-config-standard).

PASS: helix-query.yaml version 1, single 'site' index covering /**, targets /query-index.json. Includes all necessary Almac-specific fields: title, description, image, lastModified, publishDate, division, category, contentType, eventDate, eventLocation, eventType, topic, expert, template.
PASS: head.html exists with Content-Security-Policy meta, viewport meta, aem.js module script, scripts.js module script, and styles.css stylesheet link.

PASS: content-filter.js uses readBlockConfig for configuration, fetches from /query-index.json, implements faceted filter dropdowns from config fields, card rendering with date/title/description/location, pagination (PAGE_SIZE=12), URL parameter state, and smooth scroll on page change.

PASS: hubspot-form.js uses IntersectionObserver to defer HubSpot script loading until block is visible in viewport. Script loaded once (hsScriptLoaded flag). Handles missing portalId/formId gracefully.

FAIL: AEM preview https://main--eds-migration--manuel-vara.aem.page/ returns HTTP 404. Admin API confirms code.status=404 (AEM CodeSync not installed/synced). No preview screenshots possible for any archetype. Visual regression is impossible.

evidence.render_strategy: curl raw HTTP + grep for framework imports
evidence.finding: PASS: No framework imports found (React, Vue, jQuery, Tailwind, Bootstrap). All blocks use vanilla ES6+ JavaScript with .js extensions and import only from ../../scripts/aem.js or ../../scripts/scripts.js. No build steps required.

summary: FAIL: Missing .eslintrc.js breaks npm run lint:js; AEM preview is unavailable (code bus 404, no DA content) making mandatory visual regression impossible. Code quality and structure are otherwise strong — all 18 blocks present, CSS lint passes, EDS patterns followed correctly.
